text: SUPERVISOR/WATCHER CONCURRENCY MECHANICS (Windows, drained from AGENTS.md 2026-06-04): (1) Every single-instance/lock guard MUST be atomic, never check-then-act. existsSync->read->decide->writeFileSync is TOCTOU: under a concurrent burst (bootstrap spawns several supervisors in the same ms per skill-load) every caller passes the check before any write, so all proceed and the duplicate happens anyway. Acquire via atomic primitive - fs.openSync(path,'wx') (O_EXCL exclusive-create, succeeds for exactly one racer) or atomic-rename - then on EEXIST read the holder and refuse-if-alive/take-over-if-stale. A check-then-write guard passes sequential testing (later boot sees prior holder) and silently fails only under concurrency; when a guard is in place and the duplicate STILL occurs, suspect non-atomicity before absence. (2) Count plugkit processes by executable Name, never command-line substring. Get-CimInstance Win32_Process | Where {$_.CommandLine -like '*plugkit-wasm-wrapper*'} also matches the query command itself (its eval string contains the literal), fabricating phantom processes (reported 8 watchers+4 supervisors when truth was 4 watchers, 0 supervisors). Constrain: Where {($_.Name -eq 'node.exe' -or $_.Name -eq 'bun.exe') -and $_.CommandLine -match 'plugkit-wasm-wrapper\.js'}. A wrong measurement is as costly as a wrong diagnosis - the phantom count made a working atomic-guard fix look unconverged across two fires. Full incident: recall 'supervisor churn TOCTOU atomic guard'.
